title: NSF Research Experience for Undergraduates (REU) – Computational Biology
summary: Join a 10-week immersive summer research program exploring the intersection of machine learning and genomics. Students will work alongside faculty mentors on real-world bioinformatics projects and present findings at a national symposium. Stipend, housing, and travel funding are provided.

title: NASA Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship (SURF)
summary: Work directly with NASA scientists and engineers on cutting-edge space exploration projects at JPL in Pasadena, California. Fellows gain hands-on experience in aerospace engineering, data science, or physics research. Full stipend and relocation assistance are included.

title: NIH Undergraduate Scholarship Program (UGSP)
summary: A highly competitive scholarship and summer research program for students from disadvantaged backgrounds committed to biomedical research careers. Scholars receive tuition funding plus paid summer research experience at the NIH campus in Bethesda, MD. Program provides mentorship, career development workshops, and networking with leading researchers.

title: UAPB Office of RIED – Undergraduate Research Assistant
institution: University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff
summary: Support the Office of Research, Innovation & Economic Development with ongoing grant-funded projects across STEM disciplines. Students assist faculty researchers with data collection, literature reviews, and lab work on campus. This paid position offers flexible scheduling and valuable research mentorship.

title: UAPB IT Department – Student Technology Associate
institution: University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff
summary: Assist the UAPB Information Technology department with helpdesk support, network maintenance, and campus technology initiatives. Gain real-world IT experience while supporting fellow students and staff. Position includes competitive pay and potential for academic credit.

title: DOE Science Undergraduate Laboratory Internship (SULI)
summary: The SULI program encourages undergraduate students to pursue STEM careers by providing research internships at DOE national laboratories. Interns work with laboratory staff scientists on projects in energy, climate, and materials science. A competitive stipend, travel reimbursement, and housing assistance are provided.

title: UAMS Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship (SURF)
institution: University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences
summary: The Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology at UAMS sponsors this 10-week fellowship designed to immerse students in graduate-level research culture through hands-on lab projects, faculty interaction, and oral and written research presentations. Fellows receive a $5,000 stipend and a personalized lab coat. Participants are assigned mentors and a study area by mid-March.

title: Rhodes College iRaST Mellon Fellowship – HBCU Student Fellows
institution: Rhodes College — Institute for Race and Social Transformation
summary: Funded by an $800,000 Andrew W. Mellon Foundation grant, this 8-week fellowship places three HBCU students at the Rhodes Institute for Regional Studies in Memphis to conduct scholarly work on racial equity, discrimination, and justice. Fellows receive a $3,250 stipend plus campus housing and faculty mentorship. Open to full-time rising juniors or seniors with a minimum 3.0 GPA.

title: 11th Annual LSUS Regional Student Scholars Forum
summary: Undergraduate and graduate students from universities across Louisiana, Arkansas, Mississippi, Oklahoma, and Texas are invited to present research and creative work at LSUS. Disciplines include STEM, Health Sciences, Business, Education, Arts & Humanities, and Social Sciences. The event is free with lunch provided, and prizes are awarded for best poster and best presentation in separate undergraduate and graduate tracks.
